ddls:
CREATE TABLE "shoe_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shoe model */,
  "brand_id" INTEGER /* ID of the brand the model belongs to */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the shoe model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the shoe model */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender the model is designed for (e.g., Men, Women, Unisex) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the shoe model (e.g., Running, Casual) */,
  "color_options" TEXT /* Color options available for the model */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the model was released */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the shoe model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shoe_models_brand_id FOREIGN KEY ("brand_id") REFERENCES brands ("brand_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "brands" (
  "brand_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each brand */,
  "brand_name" TEXT /* Name of the brand */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the brand */,
  "country_of_origin" TEXT /* Country where the brand is headquartered */,
  "website_url" TEXT /* URL of the brand's official website */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("brand_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "size_charts" (
  "size_chart_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each size chart */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shoe model the size chart belongs to */,
  "us_men" REAL /* US Men's size */,
  "us_women" REAL /* US Women's size */,
  "uk" REAL /* UK size */,
  "eu" REAL /* EU size */,
  "jpn_cm" REAL /* Japanese (cm) size */,
  "custom_chart_name" TEXT /* Name of the custom size chart */,
  "valid_from" TEXT /* Start date of the size chart's validity */,
  "valid_to" TEXT /* End date of the size chart's validity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("size_chart_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_size_charts_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES shoe_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory" (
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory entry */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shoe model the inventory belongs to */,
  "size_chart_id" INTEGER /* ID of the size chart the inventory belongs to */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of shoes in stock */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the inventory is stored (e.g., Warehouse, Store) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time the inventory was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_size_chart_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_chart_id") REFERENCES size_charts ("size_chart_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES shoe_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who placed the order */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shoe model ordered */,
  "size_chart_id" INTEGER /* ID of the size chart ordered */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date the order was placed */,
  "total" REAL /* Total cost of the order */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the shoe model ordered */,
  "shipping_address" TEXT /* Shipping address for the order (if different from the customer's default address) */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Payment method used for the order (e.g., Credit Card, PayPal) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_size_chart_id FOREIGN KEY ("size_chart_id") REFERENCES size_charts ("size_chart_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES shoe_models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Shipping address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the customer */,
  "preferred_language" TEXT /* Preferred language of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order the status belongs to */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the order (e.g., pending, shipped, delivered) */,
  "status_date" TEXT /* Date the status was updated */,
  "expected_delivery_date" TEXT /* Expected delivery date for the order */,
  "updated_by" TEXT /* User who updated the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_status_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shipping" (
  "shipping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shipping entry */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order the shipping belongs to */,
  "carrier" TEXT /* Shipping carrier (e.g., USPS, UPS, FedEx) */,
  "tracking_number" TEXT /* Tracking number for the shipment */,
  "delivery_date" TEXT /* Date the shipment was delivered */,
  "shipping_cost" REAL /* Cost of shipping */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("shipping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shipping_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "returns" (
  "return_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each return entry */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the order the return belongs to */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the return */,
  "return_date" TEXT /* Date the return was initiated */,
  "return_status" TEXT /* Status of the return (e.g., pending, approved, rejected) */,
  "refund_amount" REAL /* Amount refunded for the return */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("return_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_returns_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, sales, customer) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to (e.g., sales, inventory, analytics) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the shoe model the analytics belongs to */,
  "sales" INTEGER /* Number of sales */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Total revenue */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date the analytics were recorded */,
  "number_of_returns" INTEGER /* Number of returns for the model */,
  "return_rate" REAL /* Return rate percentage for the model */,
  "average_rating" REAL /* Average rating of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES shoe_models ("model_id")
)
